diagrams.title: Singly Linked List Node Structure and Insertion at Beginning
diagrams.description: Shows the structure of a node with data and next pointer, followed by the step-by-step process of inserting a new node at the beginning
elements: Node box with two parts: DATA and NEXT
diagrams.purpose: Visualize how nodes are connected and how insertion at beginning works by redirecting the head pointer

diagrams.title: Deletion Operation in Singly Linked List
diagrams.description: Illustrates the three cases of deletion: from beginning, from middle, and from end
elements: Case 1 - Delete head: HEAD→[A]→[B]→[C] becomes HEAD→[B]→[C]
Case 2 - Delete middle: [A]→[B]→[C]→[D] after deleting B becomes [A]→[C]→[D]
Arrow showing previous node's next pointer redirecting
diagrams.purpose: Understand how pointers are manipulated during deletion to maintain list continuity

diagrams.title: Traversal Process Flowchart
diagrams.description: Flowchart showing how to traverse and display all elements in a singly linked list
elements: If yes: Display 'List Empty' and STOP
diagrams.purpose: Learn the systematic approach to visiting each node exactly once during traversal
